name: Orange County Public Schools
districtFact: Ninth largest school district in the United States, serving Orlando and all of Orange County, Florida.

about.title: About the District
about.content: Orange County Public Schools (OCPS) is the ninth-largest school district in the United States, with roughly 208,000 students across more than 200 schools within a single Florida county. The district covers the full Orlando metro: the tourist corridor along International Drive, older suburbs like Winter Park and Ocoee, fast-growing communities like Apopka and Horizon West, and the Lake Nona medical corridor in the southeast. The parent population includes a significant share of theme park, hospitality, and events-industry workers on hourly or shift-based schedules — which pushes holiday childcare demand notably higher around every school break and Early Release day.

about.title: What Makes the OCPS Calendar Distinctive
about.content: The OCPS school year runs from early August through late May — 180 days as required by Florida law. Early Release days are embedded roughly once per month; schools dismiss 1–2 hours early for teacher development, with campus-specific times that differ between elementary and secondary levels. Spring Break falls in mid-March, one week before Broward, Miami-Dade, and Palm Beach County schools — a gap that affects hotel rates and park wait times during that window. Hurricane season overlaps with the first quarter of school (August–November); the district builds buffer days into the calendar, so a closure or two typically doesn't push the end date later. The School Choice and magnet application cycle runs November through January — a window that catches many newcomers who assume enrollment can wait until after they've settled.

content: Early Release days are on the OCPS district calendar but dismissal times vary by school level. Elementary students leave 1–2 hours early; middle and high schools follow modified block schedules. Check your specific campus page on ocps.net — the district-wide calendar does not always show campus-level times.

OCPS School Choice and magnet applications open in November and close in late January for most programs. Missing the January window typically means a waitlist placement; for competitive programs like IB and dual-language, waitlist movement before the school year begins is not guaranteed. If you're relocating, the district accepts provisional applications before you have a confirmed address.

Spring Break in mid-March is one week before South Florida districts. Theme parks are busy during OCPS break but the following week — when Broward, Miami-Dade, and Palm Beach release — tends to see higher crowds. If you're visiting Disney or Universal during OCPS Spring Break, you're catching the upswing, not the peak.

Hurricane season runs June through November, overlapping with the first quarter of school. OCPS pre-builds makeup days into the calendar; a one or two-day closure from a tropical storm typically does not push the last day of school later. Schools announce closures the evening before or by 5 AM on the day of the event.

districtFaqs.q: When does the OCPS school year start and end?
districtFaqs.a: Orange County Public Schools typically begins the school year in early August and ends in late May, for a total of 180 instructional days as required by Florida law. Exact dates vary year to year based on the district board's calendar adoption. Check the official OCPS calendar at ocps.net for the confirmed first and last day for the current school year.

OCPS Spring Break typically falls in the third week of March — approximately one week before Spring Break for Broward County, Miami-Dade County, and Palm Beach County schools, which usually break the fourth week of March. The earlier OCPS window means Central Florida theme parks are entering their busy season during OCPS break, but the week after (when South Florida releases) generally sees higher attendance.

districtFaqs.q: What are OCPS Early Release days?
districtFaqs.a: Orange County Public Schools schedules Early Release days approximately once per month throughout the school year. On these days, schools dismiss students 1–2 hours before the normal end time while teachers use the remaining time for professional development. Dismissal times differ between elementary, middle, and high school campuses — so households with children at multiple grade levels may have different pickup windows. The specific schedule is listed on each school's page at ocps.net.

districtFaqs.q: How does OCPS handle school closures from hurricanes or tropical storms?
districtFaqs.a: OCPS typically announces storm-related closures the evening before or by 5 AM on the day of the event. The district builds buffer days into the school calendar specifically for weather disruptions — so a one or two-day tropical closure generally does not extend the school year beyond the official end date. Multiple closures in a single season may require makeup days at the end of the year, but this has been uncommon for inland Orange County, which is largely shielded from direct hurricane landfalls.

districtFaqs.q: How does OCPS School Choice work, and when do I apply?
districtFaqs.a: OCPS runs one of Florida's larger School Choice and magnet program systems, with options including STEM academies, IB programs, performing arts magnets, and dual-language immersion (Spanish, Portuguese). The application window for the following school year opens in November and closes in late January. Oversubscribed programs fill seats by lottery; remaining applicants go to a waitlist. Submit applications through the OCPS Choice and Magnet Programs portal on ocps.net. Applications submitted after the January deadline are typically placed directly on the waitlist.

districtFaqs.q: Does OCPS have a Fall Break?
districtFaqs.a: No. Orange County Public Schools does not schedule a multi-day Fall Break. The calendar includes individual teacher workdays and student holidays in October, but there is no extended fall recess comparable to what some other large Florida districts offer. Thanksgiving Break is the primary multi-day break in the fall semester.

OCPS schedules teacher workdays throughout the school year, typically one or two per quarter. On these days teachers report to school for planning or professional development, but students do not attend. Some workdays are adjacent to student holidays and extend a break by one day. The specific teacher workday dates are listed on the OCPS district calendar; they are not always highlighted in third-party calendar apps that sync school schedules.

districtFaqs.q: How do I find which OCPS school my child is zoned for?
districtFaqs.a: OCPS assigns students to schools based on home address. The district provides an address lookup tool through its website that identifies your assigned elementary, middle, and high school. If you prefer a school outside your attendance zone, you can apply through the School Choice program during the annual November–January window. Zone boundaries do occasionally shift when new schools open or population changes in a corridor, so verify your assignment after any move within Orange County.

livingHere.intro: Orange County's economy centers on tourism, hospitality, and entertainment — which means the OCPS school calendar interacts with daily life in ways that don't apply in most large US districts. Early Release timing, School Choice deadlines, and hurricane season are all more consequential here than the calendar headline dates alone suggest.

highlights.label: School Choice and Magnet Programs
highlights.detail: OCPS offers one of Florida's larger magnet and School Choice networks: STEM academies, IB programs, performing arts magnets, and dual-language immersion in Spanish and Portuguese. Applications open in November and close in late January; oversubscribed programs fill by lottery and waitlist offers before school starts aren't guaranteed. Arriving after January typically means the neighborhood school for the first year. The district accepts provisional applications from incoming residents without a confirmed local address — which matters if program access factors into the relocation decision.

highlights.label: Theme Park Employment and Childcare
highlights.detail: Disney World, Universal, and SeaWorld collectively employ a substantial portion of the OCPS parent population — many on hourly, shift-based, or seasonal contracts. Early Release days and school holidays fall at moments when the parks are also busy, driving holiday childcare demand up sharply. After-school programs and camp slots book faster in Orange County than in most comparably sized districts. If your household includes a shift-based hospitality worker, build childcare plans for each school holiday early in the school year rather than close to the break.

highlights.label: Hurricane Season Overlap
highlights.detail: School starts in early August, and hurricane season runs through November — the first quarter unfolds entirely within the active weather window. Central Florida sits far enough inland that direct landfalls are uncommon, but tropical flooding from storms making landfall on either coast can reach Orange County. OCPS pre-builds buffer days into the calendar; in most years, closures total zero to two days and don't affect the final school date. Closure announcements typically come the evening before or by 5 AM on the day of the storm.

highlights.label: Lake Nona Medical City
highlights.detail: Southeast Orange County has grown significantly around a planned medical and research campus anchored by UCF College of Medicine, Nemours Children's Hospital, and a VA medical center. Large corporate campuses including United Healthcare and the USTA national campus have followed. Households relocating for healthcare or research employment increasingly settle in this corridor, which now has its own growing cluster of OCPS elementary and middle campuses.

highlights.label: Early Release Scheduling
highlights.detail: OCPS schedules Early Release days roughly once per month. Elementary and secondary campuses have different dismissal windows on these days. For households managing pickups across multiple grade levels, the time gap between elementary and middle/high dismissal is enough to require separate arrangements — the district-wide calendar lists the date but not the campus-specific times.

highlights.label: Spring Break at the Parks
highlights.detail: OCPS Spring Break falls one week before Broward, Miami-Dade, and Palm Beach schools release. Theme parks are busy during OCPS break but see higher crowds the following week when South Florida follows. Residents visiting during OCPS Spring Break are catching the front end of the surge — not the peak.

relatedDistricts.name: Miami-Dade County Public Schools
relatedDistricts.reason: Largest district in Florida; Spring Break typically falls one week after OCPS, making the two schedules directly relevant for theme park crowd timing and South Florida travel planning.

relatedDistricts.name: Broward County Public Schools
relatedDistricts.reason: Spring Break one week after OCPS; Broward and OCPS together define the two-week Central and South Florida spring break surge for Orlando-area tourism.

relatedDistricts.name: Palm Beach County School District
relatedDistricts.reason: Third of the three major South Florida districts whose Spring Break (one week after OCPS) coincides with the annual peak week at Central Florida theme parks.
